children.name: Scopus
children.description: A monotypic genus of medium-sized wading bird instantly recognisable by its hammer-shaped head and backward-pointing crest. The Hamerkop is known for building one of the largest and most elaborate nests of any bird — a massive domed stick structure up to two metres across that can support the weight of a person. It is considered a magical or ominous bird in many African cultures.
